Entity_Description: Coldstream is a district municipality in the North Okanagan region of British Columbia, immediately southeast of the City of Vernon. The 2021 Census recorded a population of 11,171. The community wraps around the northern end of Kalamalka Lake, famed for the turquoise "Lake of Many Colours" hues its marl-rich water produces each summer. Coldstream's identity traces to the historic Coldstream Ranch, where Lord Aberdeen planted some of the Okanagan's first commercial orchards in the 1890s. Today it remains largely agricultural, with orchards and acreages protected under the Agricultural Land Reserve alongside rural residential neighbourhoods.

Short_Description: Kalamalka Lake glows an unmistakable turquoise below Coldstream each summer, a marl-rich "Lake of Many Colours" that draws swimmers to Kalavista and the beaches of Cosens Bay. But the district is more than its shoreline: orchard acreages climb the benchlands, the historic Coldstream Ranch still farms the valley floor, and rural properties stretch east toward Lavington. Long_Description:
Locals call Kalamalka Lake the "Lake of Many Colours," and its shifting bands of turquoise and sapphire — the product of marl settling out of the water each summer — set the tone for the whole district of Coldstream. The community wraps the lake's northern end, where lakeshore view homes at Kalavista and the beaches of Cosens Bay in Kalamalka Lake Provincial Park sit within minutes of working farmland. That contrast defines the place: this is one of the North Okanagan's most rural municipalities, where orchard acreages and grazing land protected by the Agricultural Land Reserve share the valley with newer residential subdivisions.

Coldstream's story is an agricultural one. The historic Coldstream Ranch still farms the valley floor, on land where Lord Aberdeen planted some of the Okanagan's first commercial orchards in the 1890s — a legacy that helped shape the fruit industry across the region. Those benchlands still carry orchards today, while the rural community of Lavington anchors the district's more forested eastern edge. On the Vernon boundary, the Middleton Mountain neighbourhood represents Coldstream's newer face, with hillside homes overlooking the valley.

This range of property types shapes what {SERVICE} looks like here. A century-old farmhouse on an ALR acreage, a lakefront build at Kalavista, and a post-2000 Middleton Mountain home each bring different demands, and {COMPANY_NAME} tailors {SERVICE} to all of them. The climate adds its own pressures: Coldstream sits in a semi-arid pocket where summer highs routinely climb past 35°C, wildfire smoke rolls through in late summer, and winters bring snow to the higher benches. Sun exposure, dust, and dry heat are constant factors that a knowledgeable local {BUSINESS_TYPE} plans around.

Proximity matters too. Coldstream shares a seamless border with Vernon, the North Okanagan's commercial hub just a few kilometres northwest, and sits within easy reach of Lake Country and Armstrong. That geography lets {COMPANY_NAME} respond quickly across the district and beyond. Whether you're along the Kalamalka lakeshore, out on a Coldstream Creek acreage, or up on Middleton Mountain, we deliver {SERVICE} that respects both this community's rural character and the standards its residents expect. To get started with {SERVICE} in Coldstream, reach {COMPANY_NAME} at {PHONE}.
Local_Facts:
- Population: 11,171 (2021 Census)
- District municipality in the North Okanagan region of British Columbia
- Borders the City of Vernon, roughly 9 km to the city centre
- Wraps the north end of Kalamalka Lake, known as the "Lake of Many Colours" for its turquoise summer hues
- Home to Kalamalka Lake Provincial Park, including the beaches at Cosens Bay
- Rooted in the historic Coldstream Ranch, where Lord Aberdeen planted early Okanagan orchards in the 1890s
- Semi-arid climate: hot, dry summers regularly topping 35°C and snowy winters on the higher benches
